iterations.hypothesis: USDC (listing 1) and USDE (listing 28) are USD-pegged stablecoins on Hyperliquid whose midprices should be tightly correlated. Transient divergences in the spread, measured as EWMA z-scores, are mean-reverting opportunities. Iteration 1 uses the NExchangeArb framework adapted for same-exchange stat-arb with moderate z=1.0 entry and 60s max hold.
changes: Initial strategy: StablecoinStatArb based on NExchangeArb pattern, entry_z=1.0, close_z=0.2, ewma_alpha=0.99, max_hold_ns=60s, size=1000
iterations.analysis: Strategy overtraded severely (90,538 fills over 3 days = 1 trade every ~3s). Root cause: the EWMA z-score with alpha=0.99 adapts to a trending/persistent spread (autocorr=0.9948 at lag-1). When z-score drops below close_z=0.2, it is because the EWMA mean caught up to the spread level, NOT because the spread reverted. This means the strategy systematically exits trades at the current elevated price rather than after a reversion, creating systematic adverse selection. pct_positive_buckets=0.0 confirms this bias is persistent across all time periods. The spread averages 1.68 bps (Security 28 premium) with high persistence — a different regime requires much longer hold periods or more selective entries.
iterations.next_action: Increase entry_z to 2.5 to enter only on larger deviations less likely to be pure trend. Set close_z=0.0 so we exit only when spread actually crosses back through the EWMA mean (true reversion), not when the mean drifts up to meet the spread. Extend max_hold_ns to 10 minutes to allow time for genuine reversion. Increase min_spread_bps to 0.5 to avoid noise trades. Keep ewma_alpha=0.99 to maintain a stable long-term mean.
